title: Mala dry-charred Brussels sprouts
desc: Halved sprouts seared cut-face down until the edges blacken, steamed through, then tossed dry with toasted ground Sichuan peppercorn and chili rather than a wet glaze. The char rebalances the sprouts' sulfur bite into nutty sweetness while the mala finish brings a numbing, fragrant heat.

title: Scallion-oil oyster mushrooms with white pepper
desc: Oyster mushrooms torn and dry-seared until the water cooks off and the edges crisp, then dressed in a hot scallion-and-ginger oil with white pepper and a bright splash of rice vinegar. The dish runs pale, clean, and allium-forward while still carrying the mushrooms' own meaty glutamate.

title: Sichuan sticky pork meatballs in doubanjiang glaze
desc: Bouncy pork meatballs built on salt-solubilized myosin, seared hard for fond, then lacquered in a glaze of fermented broad-bean paste, rock sugar, and soy reduced to a glossy cling. The doubanjiang carries fermented-chili heat and deep glutamate while Chinkiang vinegar off the heat snaps the sweetness back into focus.

title: Myosin extraction
desc: Salt a ground or minced protein, then work it hard in one direction until the mass turns tacky and pulls against the spoon. The salt solubilizes myosin, which shears into aligned strands and cross-links on cooking into the springy, water-holding gel that defines a bouncy meatball, fish ball, or sausage.

title: Ube halaya brown butter sugar cookies
desc: The brown butter sugar cookie rebuilt around ube halaya: toasted milk solids from the butter meet the caramelized condensed milk of the jam, cornstarch buffers the jam moisture, and the pan-bang trick still cracks a rippled, sugar-shelled violet top.

title: Ube halaya thumbprint cookies
desc: Brown butter shortbread with a coconut-rolled edge and a well of ube halaya baked into the center, then refreshed with a second spoon of cold jam so the violet reads vivid against the toasted crumb. Halaya is already a jam, which makes it the native filling for a thumbprint.

title: Ube halaya white chocolate cookies
desc: A brown butter drop cookie built on ube halaya with pools of white chocolate. The white chocolate is the deliberate partner: cocoa butter and milk solids with no cocoa to compete, so the violet yam stays the headline while the chocolate adds creamy, sweet, browning pockets.

title: White chocolate
desc: Confection of cocoa butter, milk solids, and sugar with no cocoa solids. The absence of cocoa mass is what makes it pale, mild, and dairy-forward, and what makes its melting and browning behavior diverge from dark chocolate.

title: Acid-forward green salad with sharp vinaigrette
desc: A simple bowl of crisp greens dressed in a deliberately sharp shallot-dijon-red-wine vinaigrette emulsified to cling, built to cut richness rather than to be a meal. The vinaigrette runs a higher acid-to-oil ratio than the textbook 3:1 (closer to 2:1) because its job is to reset the palate between bites of seared fish or buttered vegetables, not to coat the leaves in fat. Shallot macerated in the vinegar softens its bite and seasons the whole dressing; dijon emulsifies and adds tang.

ingredients.item: crisp mixed greens (romaine, butter lettuce, frisée, or arugula)
fresh herbs (parsley, chives, tarragon, or dill), torn

title: Charred broccoli with garlic-anchovy crunch
desc: Broccoli seared hard in a dry pan until the florets catch deep char, steam-finished briefly under a lid, then dressed with a crumb of panko fried in olive oil with sliced garlic, anchovy, and chili. The broccoli supplies sweet-bitter brassica char; the crumb supplies the savory, crunchy, glutamate-heavy counterpoint that turns a plain green vegetable into a side with structure. The whole dish runs on dry heat: no boiling, no steaming until the very end, so the broccoli browns instead of going gray and sulfurous.

title: Chermoula grilled tuna steak
desc: A regular fish-counter tuna steak marinated briefly in chermoula, the North African herb-and-spice paste of cilantro, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, and lemon, then grilled hard and finished cooked-through. The paste does double duty: a short marinade seasons and lightly cures the surface, and a second spoonful of raw chermoula stirred into oil dresses the fish off the grill. No sashimi grade needed, the tuna is cooked to a moist medium and the bright, garlicky, cumin-warm paste keeps the lean flesh from reading dry.

title: Ginger-scallion seared tuna
desc: Sashimi-grade tuna seared bare and fast, sliced across the grain, and finished Cantonese-style with a fresh ginger-scallion relish bloomed by smoking-hot oil poured over it at the table. The technique is borrowed wholesale from steamed Cantonese fish: raw minced ginger and scallion sit on the fish, and a ladle of smoking neutral oil hits them to release their aromatics in a sizzle, the gentlest, cleanest way to dress a delicate protein. Here it lands on seared-rare tuna, with a light soy drizzle underneath.

ingredients.item: neutral oil (high smoke point: refined avocado, rice bran, or grapeseed)
sashimi-grade tuna loin, in two blocks

title: Gochujang-glazed seared tuna
desc: Sashimi-grade tuna blocks brushed with a thin gochujang glaze cut with rice vinegar and honey, seared fast and hot so the sugars in the glaze lacquer and lightly char while the center stays cool and ruby, then sliced and finished with toasted sesame and scallion. The Korean sweet-spicy-fermented register applied with restraint: the glaze is a thin coat that caramelizes on the surface, not a braising sauce, so the fish reads bright and clean rather than heavy.

ingredients.item: sashimi-grade tuna loin, in two blocks
neutral oil (high smoke point: refined avocado, rice bran, or grapeseed)
scallion, sliced thin on the bias

title: Hard-roasted cauliflower with lemon and chili
desc: Cauliflower florets tossed in oil and roasted on a preheated sheet pan at 230 °C until the cut faces build a deep brown Maillard crust and the loose bits char to crisp, then finished hot off the pan with lemon zest and juice, chili flakes, and a shower of parsley. The dish is a single sweet-nutty-bitter-bright chord: the high dry heat caramelizes the brassica sugars and shuts down the sulfurous off-notes that plague boiled cauliflower, and the lemon-and-chili finish keeps the richness sharp.

title: Seared cremini and white mushrooms with garlic and thyme
desc: Cremini or white button mushrooms dry-seared in a hot uncrowded pan until their water boils off and the cut faces brown deep, then finished with butter, smashed garlic, thyme, and a splash of soy and lemon. The whole cook is one discipline: drive the water out before adding fat, so the mushrooms brown and turn meaty instead of stewing pale and rubbery. The soy adds glutamate that compounds the mushrooms own savory depth, and the lemon keeps the butter from reading flat.

title: Sesame-crusted seared rare tuna
desc: A thick block of sashimi-grade tuna pressed into a coat of toasted white and black sesame, then seared 30 to 45 seconds per face in a smoking-hot pan so the crust toasts and the interior stays cool, ruby, and translucent. Sliced across the grain and served with a soy-yuzu-ginger dipping sauce. The whole cook is a race against the clock: the window between a perfect rare center and a chalky overcooked one is measured in seconds, and the sesame coat is both a flavor layer and the visual signal that the surface is browning while the core is protected.

ingredients.item: fresh ginger, grated to a pulp
scallion, sliced thin on the bias
sashimi-grade tuna loin, in two blocks
neutral oil (high smoke point: refined avocado, rice bran, or grapeseed)

title: Beet greens and feta filo pie
desc: A Greek hortopita built on beet greens: the greens wilted and squeezed bone-dry, bound with feta, scallion, dill, and egg, then sealed between buttered-and-oiled filo layers and baked to a shattering golden crust. The squeeze-dry step is the whole dish; wet greens steam the filo soggy, dry greens let it shatter. The feta and egg carry the calcium that pairs with the beet-green oxalate, and a hit of lemon zest in the filling keeps the iron-mineral leaves bright under the rich pastry.

ingredients.item: beet greens (the leaves and stems off 4 to 5 medium beets)

title: White beans braised with beet greens
desc: A Tuscan bean-and-greens braise: cannellini and their starchy liquor simmered with a soffritto of onion, garlic, and a Parmesan rind, then folded with staged beet greens (stems first, leaves wilted on top) until the broth self-thickens into something between soup and stew. Built on the calcium-pairing logic the beet-green oxalate wants: the Parmesan rind and the grated finish supply the dietary calcium, the cannellini supply the creamy body, and a lemon-and-vinegar finish lifts the iron-mineral leaves. Eaten in a shallow bowl with grilled bread to mop the liquor.

ingredients.item: beet greens (the leaves and stems off 3 to 4 medium beets)
cooked cannellini beans, drained (about 2 cans, or 200 g dry cooked)

title: Cannellini beans
desc: Large white kidney-shaped beans (Phaseolus vulgaris), the Tuscan house bean. Thin-skinned, creamy, mild, with a high surface-to-interior ratio that drinks braising liquid and turns silky. The structural legume of central Italian soups, braises, and bean-and-greens plates.

title: Filo pastry
desc: Paper-thin sheets of unleavened wheat dough (flour, water, a little oil and vinegar) stretched to translucency and stacked dry. Brushed with fat and layered, it bakes into the shattering laminated crust of Greek, Turkish, and Levantine pies. Bought frozen; the dough itself is a stretching feat, not a kitchen quick-job.

title: Jungle curry paste (prik gaeng pa)
desc: A pounded-fresh Central Thai jungle curry paste built on soaked dried red chilies, lemongrass, galangal, and the two aromatics that define the style: fingerroot (krachai) and a backbone of white pepper. No roasted seed spice and no sweetness, so the paste reads fierce, herbaceous, and clean rather than rounded like a red curry paste. The base for a coconut-free forest curry.

ingredients.item: dried long red chilies (prik chee fah / Thai guajillo type), seeded
lemongrass, white part only, sliced thin
fresh galangal, peeled and sliced thin
fresh coriander root (or lower cilantro stems)

title: Jungle curry with catfish (kaeng pa pla duk)
desc: A coconut-free Central Thai forest curry: jungle curry paste bloomed in oil, slackened with stock rather than coconut milk, loaded with Thai eggplant, yardlong beans, and bamboo, then sharpened with the two aromatics that define the style, fingerroot matchsticks and whole stems of fresh green peppercorn. Cured catfish steaks poach in the thin, fierce broth, their firm flake holding while the krachai cuts the freshwater back-note. Hot, herbaceous, and bracingly clean where a coconut curry is rich.

ingredients.item: catfish steaks (skin-on, bone-in, 2 to 3 cm thick)
jungle curry paste (store-bought, or red curry paste)
yardlong beans, cut in 4 cm lengths
fresh green peppercorns, on the stem

title: Jungle curry with chicken (kaeng pa gai)
desc: The coconut-free Central Thai forest curry built on chicken thigh: jungle curry paste bloomed hard in oil, slackened with stock, then simmered with thigh meat whose collagen lends the thin broth a little body it otherwise lacks. Thai eggplant, baby corn, and yardlong beans carry the bulk; fingerroot matchsticks and whole stems of fresh green peppercorn carry the identity; a finishing fistful of holy basil and sliced chili carries the heat. Bracingly hot, herbaceous, and clean.

ingredients.item: jungle curry paste (store-bought, or red curry paste)
boneless skinless chicken thigh, sliced 1 cm thick
yardlong beans, cut in 4 cm lengths
fresh green peppercorns, on the stem

title: Fingerroot (krachai)
desc: Slender finger-like rhizome of Boesenbergia rotunda, peppery and medicinal with a faint ginseng bitterness. The defining aromatic of Thai jungle curry and freshwater-fish dishes; not interchangeable with ginger or galangal.
